title: Thelypteris tandikatensis

The species is also known as a member of the order Polypodiales. The classification of this plant is further divided into class Polypodiopsida and subclass Polypodiidae. intro: Thelypteris tandikatensis, also known as Tandikat fern, is a species of fern within the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris tandikatensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, this plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to make herbal teas and topical creams to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation.
